mnemonics.title: Injective vs Surjective
mnemonics.content: INjective: Different inputs → NO shared outputs. SURjective: SURvey all outputs (cover entire codomain).

mnemonics.title: Bijective Check
mnemonics.content: Bijection = One-to-One AND Onto. Remember: 'Bi' means 'two' - two properties required.

mnemonics.title: Horizontal Line Test
mnemonics.content: Horizontal line cuts graph: 0 or 1 time = one-to-one; 2+ times = NOT one-to-one.

mnemonics.title: Domain Codomain Range
mnemonics.content: DReam of Clean Rooms: Domain (inputs), Range (actual outputs), Codomain (possible outputs).

mnemonics.title: Inverse Exists
mnemonics.content: INVerses Need INjectivity + SURjectivity = Bijectivity. 'IN-SUR-BI' helps remember.

acronyms.usage: The set A in f: A → B where function is defined
The set B in f: A → B that contains the range
Subset of codomain containing all f(x) values

visualTips.visualization: Think of a marriage scenario where each person in group A marries exactly one person in group B, and no two people from A marry the same person in B.
Imagine distributing gifts (codomain) to children (domain) where every gift must be given to some child.
Think of perfect pairing like socks - each sock has exactly one matching sock, no leftovers.
Imagine scanning across a graph with a horizontal laser beam. If beam ever hits 2+ points, it's NOT one-to-one.
Think of a function as a machine: input goes in → transforms → output comes out. Same input always gives same output.

analogies.analogy: A function is like a vending machine: you press a button (input), get a specific item (output). Same button always gives same item.
Like fingerprint - each person's fingerprint is unique. No two people have identical fingerprints.
Like a bus route that covers all stops - every stop on the route (codomain) is visited by the bus.
Like matching dance partners - each person has exactly one partner, and everyone is paired.
Like undo button in software - if f transforms x to y, f⁻¹ transforms y back to x.
Codomain is like the menu (all possible dishes), Range is what customers actually ordered (subset of menu).
